content.full_text:
Stanley E. Steele
13555 Plantation Way
Moreno Valley, CA 92360
(714) 242-1944

March 30, 1990

Jill Stecker, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Ste. 600
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Request for Formal Written Advice from
Stanley E. Steele, Planning Commissioner,
City of Moreno Valley

Dear Ms. Stecker:

I am currently an appointed Planning Commissioner for the
City of Moreno Valley. I am writing to you or to whomever
this matter may be assigned, to request your written advice
regarding my participation in a proposed project which will be
coming before the Planning Commission for public hearing,
currently scheduled for May 3, 1990.

I enclose with this letter a map showing the location of
my residence, and also showing the boundaries of the project
in relation to the location of my residence. You will
note from the map the residence is located about 900 feet from
the boundary of the project closest to my property. My
property is owned in fee by myself and my wife.

I have reviewed the provisions of Section 18702.3 of the
Fair Political Practice Commission Regulations, dealing with
ownership interest in real property indirectly involved in the
decision, and whether it constitutes a material financial
effect, and am uncertain whether I will be barred from voting,
and seek your opinion and any further guidance in this regard
as you may wish to offer.

The proposed project is entitled the "Moreno Highlands
Specific Plan" which is described in the Executive Summary of
the Draft Specific Plan as a new 3,038-acre master planned
community situated in the eastern area of the City of Moreno
Valley, located entirely within the City's sphere of
influence, with approximately 1,800 acres located within the
boundaries of the City, and the balance located outside of the
City boundaries in the County. The area of the proposed
project is largely unimproved land, and the developer is

Jill Stecker, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
March 30, 1990
Page 2


proposing to provide approximately 554 acres of planned
business center and approximately 8,458 residential dwellings
"linked together through a comprehensive network of parks,
greenbelts, golf course and open space." The proposed
development is comparable in size to certain other nearby
planned communities in the City, and which have previously
been approved. The difference in the proposed project is that
it is proposed to contain a significant business center and a
major community park. I understand that part of the proposal
includes, in addition to the specific plan, a proposed
annexation and development agreement, and the underlying
zoning of the area will be changed to a specific planned zone
designed for the project. I also enclose with this letter a
copy of an illustrative plan and land use plan included in the
applicant's summary of the project, and which shows the land
uses generally proposed and the street layout, which, when
compared to the enclosed map showing my residence, will more
fully provide you with the layout of the project as it will be
situated in the general area where my residence is located.

Also because the project will differ from what was
generally proposed in the area by the City's general plan, a
general plan amendment is being processed for the applicant,
as well. The enclosed Executive Summary of the project,
together with a portion of the introductory summary and
project description, including summary of impacts, from the
draft environmental impact report submitted with the project,
more fully describes the project, and includes project
impacts, including the fact that the proposed project would
remove most of the present (Rural Residential) designation and
a majority of the R1 designation (one unit to the acre),
double the area of R2 (2 units per acre), increase the area of
R3 (3 units per acre) more than 20 times, decrease the P1 area
by 85%, introduce more dense residential uses and new
commercial, business park, public and open space land use
categories. In effect, the project would be more homogeneous
or consistent with other areas of the City, and would preclude
the retention of a rural eastern end (where the project is
located) and would preclude a more intense industrial
development of the portion of the site designated as P1 in the
current general plan.

My current residence is located on a relatively large lot
(approximately 1/2 acre), fronting on a small cul-de-sac
street as shown on the enclosed map. My wife and I reside in
our home, and it is not rented out and we do not anticipate
renting it in the foreseeable future. Since our lot and the
street as shown on the enclosed map.

Jill Stecker, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
March 30, 1990
Page 3


adjacent residential lots have been subdivided as a large lot
development, it would not appear that the project will have an
effect on the current use or would likely result in a change
of use. The character of our immediate neighborhood is set
and would not likely be changed, although obviously the
character of the project site, itself, will change
substantially. Since we are on a cul-de-sac, the effect of
traffic on our street would not likely be significant. I
don't believe the noise levels will significantly increase on
our lot, and I do not anticipate a negative or positive effect
with respect to view, privacy or air omissions as these items
affect our property.

I have not hired an independent real estate appraiser to
appraise our property (before and after the project approval,
if approved), and do not anticipate going through the expense
of doing so, and the City does not have a policy of providing
appraisers under such circumstances. Land values generally
have been increasing and I anticipate will continue increase
in the City, and although I am not absolutely positive, I am
not aware that the project (whether it is approved or not
approved) will affect the value of my property (either up or
down) by $10,000 or more, not considering the general
inflationary property valuation trends in the City.

I believe if I were to place my residence on the market
today, it would likely sell for a price between $200,000 and
$250,000.

I will be happy to provide you with any further
information you feel is necessary. I can be reached at the
above phone number if anything further is needed, and I would
sincerely appreciate hearing from you prior to the
commencement of the hearings on this project.

I hope I have provided you with enough information to
give me specific advice concerning whether or not I should
participate in the project. As a supplement to that, I would
appreciate receiving any specific suggestions or guidance you
may be able to give me to assist in determining both for this
project and other projects which may come up, whether they

Jill Stecker, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
March 30, 1990
Page 4


would have a material financial effect such as to preclude my
participation.

     Thank you kindly for your consideration.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Stanley E. Steele


A:MV225

sections.question_synthetic: A Planning Commissioner for the City of Moreno Valley seeks formal written advice on whether his property ownership approximately 900 feet from a proposed 3,038-acre master-planned community project constitutes a material financial effect that would bar his participation in the Planning Commission's review of the project under FPPC Regulations Section 7a702.3.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response/advice letter. Document contains no QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections in the formal sense - rather, it is the applicant's letter requesting advice. The question_synthetic was synthesized from the content of the request. Document includes extensive enclosures (map, project plans, executive summary) referenced but not included in the text provided.

embedding.summary: Stanley E. Steele, an appointed Planning Commissioner for the City of Moreno Valley, requests formal written advice regarding whether his participation in reviewing the proposed Moreno Highlands Specific Plan would create a conflict of interest, given that his residence is located approximately 900 feet from the project boundary. He provides detailed information about the project scope, his property characteristics, and his analysis of potential impacts to his property value.
